title: calea punctata
intro: Calea punctata, also known as Mexican damiana or yerba de la pastora, is a plant species native to Mexico and Central America, belonging to the family Asteraceae. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, which include analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing anxiety and stress, improving sleep quality, and boosting the immune system. Calea punctata can be prepared as herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is also used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments.
